diagnostic_explanation: Both reviewers kept the same unsupported label and cited no evidence, so there is no citation difference to evaluate. The substantive difference is how they diagnose why the claim is unsupported. Option 1 treats the record as simply lacking support for choosing an advanced architecture, using insufficient_evidence. Option 2 interprets the available evidence as actively cutting against the claim: the junior-team constraint, stalled complex design, and success of the teachable/simple design together function as contrary evidence, motivating counterexample_ignored. Given the supplied evidence bundle, these are complementary but distinct framings of the same unsupported outcome, with option 2 relying on a stronger contradiction-based reading rather than mere absence of support. Confidence does not differ because both are high.
